name: link
description: Structural links define how structural nodes attach to zones or other structural objects. Link properties and attachment modes control normal/shear transfer and sliding behavior.

notes: 'structure link property' is followed by a DOF selector ('x', 'y', or 'rotation' for FLAC2D), then per-DOF sub-keywords like 'attach', 'model', 'area', 'stiffness', etc.
FLAC2D nodes have 3 DOFs: x, y, rotation. FLAC3D adds 'z', 'rotation-x', 'rotation-y' / 'rotation-z'.
Probe live with 'structure link property x ?' to see per-DOF sub-keywords.

concepts.name: attachment
concepts.description: Defines the target object and degree-of-freedom relationship for a structural node.

concepts.name: link model
concepts.description: Defines the mechanical response used by a link in each degree of freedom.

concepts.name: sliding
concepts.description: Controls whether the attachment location can migrate during large-strain motion.

workflow_hints: Most structure creation commands create required links automatically.
Use explicit link commands when you need nondefault attachment behavior.
List links after structure creation when troubleshooting unexpected support-grid interaction.
